report_name: ShinyHunters Leaks 1.4 Million Udemy User Records, Including Financial Data, After Failed Extortion

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A financially motivated cybercrime group that publicly leaked a database of 1.4 million records from Udemy after a failed extortion attempt. The group specializes in large-scale data theft for extortion.
threat_actors.goals: To extort money from victim organizations by threatening to leak stolen sensitive data.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: The threat actors exfiltrated and publicly released the 1.4 million record database on their dark web leak site after the extortion demand was not met.

attack_patterns.name: Financial Theft
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor leaked sensitive instructor financial payout information, including PayPal accounts and bank details, creating a direct risk of financial fraud and theft.

attack_patterns.name: Gather Victim Identity Information
attack_patterns.description: The threat actors exfiltrated a database containing extensive Personally Identifiable Information (PII) for 1.4 million users and instructors, including full names, email addresses, physical addresses, and phone numbers.

identities.name: Udemy, Inc.
identities.description: An online education platform that suffered a major data breach, resulting in the leak of 1.4 million user and instructor records.

infrastructure.name: ShinyHunters Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by the ShinyHunters group to publicly release the stolen Udemy database after the failed extortion attempt.
